name: Vegeta (HTTP Load Testing)

auth.notes: Pass headers for auth: -header 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN'

performance.notes: Go-based; constant request rate with precise timing

agent_readiness.notes: Constant-rate HTTP load testing tool. echo 'GET https://target' | vegeta attack -rate=100 -duration=30s | vegeta report. Rate in requests/second (precise). vegeta report outputs: mean, p95, p99, success rate. vegeta plot (HTML latency graph). Binary output piped between commands. Go library API available. Targets file for multiple endpoints. Compares to: k6 (JS scripting), locust (Python scenarios). Best for SLO verification at precise QPS.

gotchas: Constant rate only (no ramp-up scenarios like k6)
Binary output format requires vegeta report/plot to read
No scripting for complex user flows
best_when: SLO verification at a specific request rate
P99 latency measurement under constant load
CI/CD performance regression checks at fixed QPS
avoid_when: Complex user flow simulation (use k6 or locust)
summary: Vegeta is the simplest precise constant-rate HTTP load tester. echo 'GET URL' | vegeta attack -rate=100 -duration=30s | vegeta report. Precise QPS control for SLO verification. P99/P95 latency metrics. HTML latency plots. Binary output pipeline design. No scripting — use k6/locust for complex flows. Best for 'does my API handle 100 RPS with p99<200ms?' validation.
